response.justification: The candidate has a solid foundation as a polyglot software engineer with roughly 16 years of professional experience and genuine entrepreneurial instincts, but falls significantly short of the Principal/Director-level AI strategy bar. AI and cloud work is extremely thin — one line about MCP tooling in a role that started approximately 14 months ago — and there is no evidence of recognized expertise in AI, strategic customer advisory experience, cloud platform design at scale, or the executive-level technical influence this role requires. The overall profile matches a strong senior-to-staff individual contributor, not a director-level AI strategist.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background spanning web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure over 16+ years, demonstrating genuine cross-domain adaptability
Hands-on AI agent and MCP tooling work at re:cinq signals current-generation builder instincts in the agentic AI space
Demonstrated entrepreneurial drive through open-source platform founding (GISCollective), community education workshops, and conference speaking
concerns: AI/ML expertise is shallow and very recent; no advanced degree and no track record of shipping AI-powered products or platforms at meaningful scale
No evidence of director-level scope: strategic customer advisory, cross-organizational technical influence, roadmap ownership, or cloud platform architecture at scale
Cloud experience is limited to AWS CDK usage in a pricing-team context, far below the cloud depth and breadth this role demands

key_factors.factor: Engineering breadth and years of tenure
